skill.name: algorithmic-art
skill.description: Creating algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter exploration. Use this when users request creating art using code, generative art, algorithmic art, flow fields, or particle systems. Create original algorithmic art rather than copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations.
skill.summary: Create algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter exploration for reproducible generative visuals.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: All 42 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate p5.js generative art tool. Network access is limited to CDN resources for p5.js library and Google Fonts required for the viewer. The static scanner confused markdown code formatting, regex operations, and template comments as security risks.

critical_findings.title: False Positive: Windows SAM Database Reference
critical_findings.description: Static scanner flagged line 508 as 'Windows SAM database' but the actual content is '// PARTICLE SYSTEM' comment in the template. Scanner confused 'SYSTEM' with Windows SAM. This is a template placeholder comment.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 508 contains template comment '// PARTICLE SYSTEM - CUSTOMIZE FOR YOUR ALGORITHM'. No Windows SAM reference exists.

critical_findings.title: False Positive: Dangerous Heuristic Combination
critical_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'Code execution + Network + Credential access' heuristic. This was triggered by benign JavaScript (regex .exec()), CDN network calls, and the word 'exec'. No credential access or malicious code exists.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: Heuristic was triggered by false positives. No credential access or malicious code execution present.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Python exec() Function
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged JavaScript regex .exec() at line 133 as 'Python exec()'. The actual code is 'const result = /^#?([a-f\d]{2})([a-f\d]{2})([a-f\d]{2})$/i.exec(hex)' which parses hex color values.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: JavaScript regex .exec() for hex color parsing, not Python's eval() equivalent.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged various lines as 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' but no cryptographic code exists. Scanner likely confused algorithmic art terminology with cryptography.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Skill contains no cryptographic code. References are to algorithmic art concepts like 'phase', 'sine waves', 'frequency' which scanner confused with crypto.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands / Shell Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged markdown backticks (`) as shell backtick execution. These are code formatting delimiters in SKILL.md documentation, not actual shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown documentation uses backticks for code formatting. No shell commands present in this skill.

low_findings.title: Legitimate: Hardcoded CDN URLs
low_findings.description: Network URLs at lines 23-26 are legitimate CDN resources for p5.js library and Google Fonts required for the interactive art viewer to function.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are standard CDN URLs for p5.js and Google Fonts - essential dependencies for the art viewer.

content.value_statement: Create unique generative art using p5.js with seeded randomness for reproducible results. Build interactive art that users can explore through parameter controls and seed navigation.
actual_capabilities: Create p5.js algorithmic art with seeded randomness for reproducibility
Build interactive HTML artifacts with parameter controls and seed navigation
Express artistic visions through computational processes and emergent behavior
Generate flow fields, particle systems, and geometric patterns
Produce gallery-quality generative art with careful parameter tuning
Export art as downloadable PNG files
limitations: Requires internet access to load p5.js library from CDN
Cannot create raster image files directly, only generates HTML/JS artifacts
Art style is limited to what p5.js can render in browser
Complex animations may impact performance on slower devices

use_cases.title: Creative Professionals
use_cases.description: Generate unique visual assets for projects, websites, or marketing materials with reproducible seeds.
use_cases.target_user: Designers, artists, and creative professionals seeking algorithmic visuals

use_cases.title: Learning Creative Coding
use_cases.description: Understand generative art principles through working examples and parameter exploration.
use_cases.target_user: Students and developers learning p5.js and creative coding

use_cases.title: Interactive Art Creation
use_cases.description: Create shareable interactive art that others can explore through parameter adjustments.
use_cases.target_user: Digital artists and educators creating interactive experiences

prompt_templates.title: Basic Art Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create algorithmic art using p5.js. Use seeded randomness to ensure reproducibility. Build an interactive HTML artifact with parameter controls.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new generative art project

prompt_templates.title: Flow Field Art
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a flow field algorithmic art piece using p5.js. Include particle systems that follow noise-driven vector fields. Add controls for particle count, noise scale, and speed.

prompt_templates.title: Geometric Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate geometric algorithmic art using recursive subdivision or circular patterns. Include seed navigation so users can explore variations.

prompt_templates.title: Interactive Explorer
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete interactive algorithmic art viewer with seed navigation (prev/next/random), parameter sliders, color pickers, and export functionality.

output_examples.input: Create algorithmic art about organic emergence with particles
output_examples.output: Generates an interactive HTML artifact with flowing particles that respond to noise fields, parameter controls for adjustment, and seed-based reproducibility.

output_examples.input: Build geometric art with recursive patterns and seed navigation
output_examples.output: Produces self-contained HTML with recursive geometric patterns, sidebar controls for exploring parameters, and buttons to navigate between seed variations.

best_practices: Always use seeded randomness (randomSeed, noiseSeed) for reproducible results
Design parameters that emerge naturally from the algorithmic philosophy
Keep all code inline in a single HTML file for portability
anti_patterns: Avoid copying existing artists' work - create original algorithmic philosophies
Do not skip the template - use viewer.html as the starting foundation
Avoid random color values - use thoughtful, harmonious palettes

faq.question: What is algorithmic art?
faq.answer: Algorithmic art is visual art created using computational algorithms. It uses mathematical processes and code to generate unique, often reproducible visual outputs.

faq.question: How does seeded randomness work?
faq.answer: Seeded randomness uses a starting number (seed) to initialize random number generators. This makes every run produce the same output, essential for reproducible art.

faq.question: Can I use this skill offline?
faq.answer: The skill requires internet access initially to load p5.js from CDN. Once loaded, the generated art can work offline in a cached browser session.

faq.question: What is the algorithmic philosophy?
faq.answer: The algorithmic philosophy is a computational worldview that guides the art creation. It describes the mathematical and aesthetic principles that the algorithm expresses.

faq.question: How do I share the generated art?
faq.answer: The output is a single self-contained HTML file. You can share it by sending the file or hosting it on any web server.

faq.question: Can I export the art as an image?
faq.answer: Yes, the interactive viewer includes a download button that exports the current canvas as a PNG file.
